nodes.name: Manual Trigger
nodes.description: Starts a workflow manually with optional user-provided data. Used when a workflow should execute on demand rather than automatically.

nodes.name: Cron Trigger
nodes.description: Starts a workflow on a recurring schedule defined by a cron expression. Useful for periodic data syncs, scheduled reports, and time-based automation.

params.cron: string — cron expression, e.g. '0 9 * * *' for daily at 09:00

nodes.name: HTTP Request
nodes.description: Performs a configurable HTTP request and returns the response. Supports GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE methods with custom headers and JSON or string body.

params.method: string — HTTP method, defaults to GET
params.body: string|object — request payload, JSON serialized when object
params.timeout: number — request timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30000

nodes.name: Conditional (IF)
nodes.description: Evaluates a condition against previous node output and routes execution to the true or false branch. Supports template interpolation of upstream node data into the condition expression.

params.condition: string — expression evaluated as boolean, e.g. '{{steps.http.body.status}} == "ok"'
